articles.title: Markets News, June 12, 2026: Indexes Close Higher on SpaceX Debut Day, Finish Up for Week; Oil Falls on Hopes of US-Iran Deal
articles.summary: S&P 500 +0.5%, Dow +0.7%, Nasdaq +0.3% on Friday. SpaceX IPO raised $75B at $135, closed at $161 (+19%). Oil fell 3.5-3.8% on Iran peace deal hopes. PPI +1.1% m/m, +6.5% y/y. Jobless claims 229K. 10Y at ~4.49%. DXY 99.78. Gold $4,230/oz. Bitcoin ~$63,600.
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500 closed at 7,431.46 (+0.5%). Dow 51,202.26 (+0.7%). Nasdaq 25,888.84 (+0.3%). SpaceX IPO: $135 IPO price, opened $150, closed above $160 (+19%), $75B raised — largest IPO in history. WTI $84.35 (-3.8%). Brent $87.33 (-3.5%). 10Y ~4.49% +2bps. PPI +1.1% monthly, +6.5% annually — largest yearly increase since Nov 2022. Jobless claims 229,000. 96% probability Fed holds rates steady per CME FedWatch. Incoming Chair Kevin Warsh. Consumer sentiment: net 54% expect unemployment to rise. Adobe -7%. Nvidia telling Chinese customers Vera AI chip could be available in China by August.

articles.title: How major US stock indexes fared Friday 6/12/2026
SpaceX (SPCX) IPO: Live updates — CNBC
articles.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) IPO: $135/share, opened $150, closed ~$161 (+19%). $75B raised — largest IPO ever. $2.1T valuation at close. 2025 revenue $18.7B, net loss $4.9B. Q1 2026 net loss $4.28B. Starlink only profitable segment. Musk became world's first trillionaire.
articles.extracted_text: IPO Price: $135. Shares: 555,555,555 + 83,333,333 overallotment. Total raised: $75B. Opening trade: $150. Day range: $150-$176.52. Close: ~$161 (+19%). After-hours: $166.85 (+3.67%). Valuation: ~$2.1T at close, intraday high ~$2.21T. Volume: 500M+ shares. 2025 revenue: $18.7B. 2025 net loss: $4.9B. Q1 2026 net loss: $4.28B. Accumulated deficit: $41.3B. Starlink 2025 revenue: $11.39B (61% of total). Starship development: >$15B to date. CFRA: Sell rating, $115 target. Damodaran: fair value ~$1.3T. Musk voting control: 82.4%. Lock-up: 366 days.

articles.title: Oil Just Fell 2% and Gold Is Dropping. Here's Why the Iran Peace Trade Doesn't Tell the Full Story
articles.summary: Brent $88.37 (-2.2% June 12), WTI $85.92 (-2%). Energy sector YTD +21.5%. Gold market price >$3,300/oz vs US govt book value $42.22/oz (7,700% gap). Oil drop is mechanical premium removal, not demand collapse. Gold's long-term structural story: central bank buying, currency debasement, debt expansion.
articles.extracted_text: Brent crude $88.37 down 2.2%. WTI $85.92 down 2%. Energy sector YTD +21.5%. US govt gold book value $42.22/oz (last updated 1973). Gold market price >$3,300/oz — 7,700% gap. Central banks net buyers for 16 consecutive years. Oil: Supply disruption premium from April-May tensions unwinding. Persian Gulf handles ~20% of global oil trade. Iran production capacity 3.5-4 million bpd.

articles.title: 2026 Taxable Fixed Income Mid-Year Outlook
articles.summary: Schwab mid-year outlook: 10Y expected in 4-4.5% range, risks to upside. Core PCE 3.3% y/y in April. Fed base case: extended pause through year-end. IG corporate yields >5%. HY yields attractive but spreads low. Favor below-benchmark duration.
articles.extracted_text: 10-year Treasury yield expected to stay mostly in 4%-4.5% range, risks skewed upside. Core PCE at 3.3% y/y in April (highest since Nov 2023). Fed base case: extended pause, holding policy rate steady through year-end 2026. Market-implied probabilities shifted from nearly three cuts by June 2027 (late Feb) to pricing one hike by May 28. IG corporate bond index average yield >5%. HY share of Ba-rated bonds >55% of index. Favor below-benchmark average duration. Upside risks: sticky inflation, fiscal concerns, rising global bond yields, elevated term premiums.

articles.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns of 'imminent' response to Israeli strike on Beirut as ceasefire deal looks shaky
articles.summary: Trump declared Iran deal 'now complete' Sunday. Iran's Supreme National Security Council warns of 'imminent' response to Israeli strike on Beirut that killed 3. Trump raged at Netanyahu: 'He has no fucking judgement.' Deal signing delayed by hours. Fox News reports electronic signing expected within 2-3 hours. Qatar negotiators flew to Tehran. US Defense Sec Hegseth: 'already doing things to ensure safe passage through Strait of Hormuz.'
articles.extracted_text: Trump Truth Social: 'This morning's attack on Beirut should not have happened... We are very close to a Deal.' Trump to Axios: 'It shook it up. It delayed the signing by a few hours... Why did Bibi have to do a fucking attack? He has no fucking judgement.' Iran Supreme National Security Council: 'The response of the fighters of Islam is imminent.' US Defense Sec Hegseth on CBS: 'We're already doing things I can't talk about on this program to ensure that safe passage [through Strait of Hormuz] happens as quickly as possible.' Fox News: Trump will ask Iran not to retaliate, expects electronic signing within 2-3 hours. Qatari negotiators fly to Tehran.

articles.title: US and Iran have agreed to wording of a deal to end their war, Pakistan's prime minister says
articles.summary: Pakistan PM: US and Iran have agreed to wording of ceasefire deal. Trump said Thursday deal is close. Deal would end hostilities and reopen Strait of Hormuz.
articles.extracted_text: Pakistan's prime minister says US and Iran agreed to wording of deal. Trump said in Oval Office Thursday deal is close. AP reports both sides confirmed wording agreement.

articles.title: CBOE Volatility Index (^VIX) Historical Data
articles.summary: VIX closed at 19.51 on June 12, down from 21.25 on June 11 and 20.10 on June 10. VIX compressed 8.2% on Friday as oil fell and geopolitical risks eased.

articles.title: U.S. Economic Calendar — MarketWatch
articles.summary: Mon: Empire State mfg, industrial production. Tue: Housing starts, import prices. Wed: Retail sales + FOMC decision (Warsh's first). Thu: Jobless claims, Philly Fed. Fri: Juneteenth — markets closed.
articles.extracted_text: Mon Jun 15: Empire State survey (forecast 13.9 vs 19.6 prior), Industrial production (+0.3% vs +0.7%), Capacity utilization (76.2 vs 76.1). Tue Jun 16: Import prices (+0.8% vs +1.9%), Housing starts (1.41M vs 1.47M), Building permits (1.42M vs 1.44M). Wed Jun 17: Retail sales (+0.5% vs +0.5%), Retail ex-autos (+0.6% vs +0.7%), FOMC decision 2:00pm, Warsh presser 2:30pm. Thu Jun 18: Jobless claims (226K vs 229K), Philly Fed (11.0 vs -0.4). Fri Jun 19: Juneteenth holiday.

articles.title: 3 Beaten Down AI Infrastructure Stocks to Buy in June — 24/7 Wall St.
articles.summary: NVDA trades 26% below 52-week high despite 85% YoY revenue growth. AVGO dropped 22% in one week on sell-the-news overreaction after AI guide missed $17B whisper number. BofA remains bullish on AI chip stocks.
articles.extracted_text: NVDA trades 26% below its 52-week high despite 85% YoY revenue growth. AVGO dropped 22% in one week on a sell-the-news overreaction. AVGO's AI guide missed the $17 billion whisper number, though analysts hold a $522 average price target.

articles.title: The 1-Minute Market Report, June 14, 2026
articles.summary: Significant rotation into small and micro caps. Value over growth for second consecutive week. Mag 7+ losing support. Blockchain, Basic Materials, Consumer Staples attracted inflows. Commodities and bonds saw outflows. Iran peace deal and 10Y Treasury rate remain critical macro catalysts.
articles.extracted_text: Last week saw significant rotation into small and micro caps, with large caps lagging and the Mag 7+ losing support. Investors shifted toward value over growth for the second consecutive week, favoring smaller, cheaper stocks over the Magnificent Seven. Blockchain, Basic Materials, and Consumer Staples attracted inflows, while commodities and bonds saw outflows amid risk-on sentiment.

articles.title: Euro Technical Forecast: EUR/USD Rebound Hits Pivotal Resistance
articles.summary: EUR/USD testing key resistance at 1.1578/98 — former support now pivot. Fed Fund Futures indicate 58% probability of at least one 25bp hike by year-end. FOMC Wednesday with Warsh's inaugural address.
articles.extracted_text: EUR/USD rebound +0.8% from monthly low now testing pivotal resistance at 1.1578/98 (61.8% Fib, May low, Jan close low). Bearish invalidation above 1.1746/75. Close below 1.1483 needed for downtrend resumption. FOMC Wednesday: Warsh's inaugural address, SEP dot plot. Fed Fund Futures: 58% probability of ≥25bp hike by year-end.

articles.title: Credit Spreads — StreetStats (June 11, 2026)
articles.summary: IG corporate bond yield 5.15%, spread 75 bps over Treasuries. HY yield 6.97%, spread 278 bps. Spreads near multi-year tights but fundamentals strong: BBB share declined to ~44%, BB share rose to ~56%. No HY defaults in May — first such month since Aug 2018.
articles.extracted_text: As of June 11, 2026: IG corporate bond yield 5.15%, spread 0.75% over Treasuries. HY corporate bond yield 6.97%, spread 2.78%. HY default rate 2.02%. LPL notes: BBB share of IG universe declined to ~44% (from 52% in 2021). BB share of HY market rose to ~56% (from 46% in 2024). No payment defaults or distressed exchanges in HY in May — first such month since August 2018.
